etymology.description: 廾(廾) (two hands) + original 口 (an object) -> holding an object with two hands; original form of 供(供) (gòng, 'to provide') -> 廿 positional adjustment -> modern 共
etymology.interpretation: The character originally depicted two hands holding an object, representing the idea of coming together to support or present something together. Over time, this concept of holding or presenting led to the abstract sense of acting 'together'.

examples.chinese: 我们一起工作(我們一起工作)，共享成果(共享成果)。
examples.english: We work together and share the results.

examples.chinese: 这次旅行我们共度了美好时光(這次旅行我們共度了美好時光)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè cì lǚxíng wǒmen gòngdùle měihǎo shíguāng。
examples.english: We spent a wonderful time together during this trip.

examples.chinese: 他们共同合作(他們共同合作)，完成了项目(完成了項目)。
examples.english: They worked together and completed the project.
